title: launchd persistence invokes suspicious tool
why_it_matters: launchd items invoking interpreters or download utilities may indicate suspicious persistence.
false_positive_notes: Enterprise agents and developer tooling may use interpreters; validate code signing, path, owner, and business purpose.
recommended_next_steps: Inspect plist contents, ProgramArguments, file hashes, signing state, and related process/network evidence.

title: Background task management item observed
why_it_matters: macOS background task management records can reveal user-level persistence and login items.
false_positive_notes: Many approved applications register background items; validate developer identity and path.
recommended_next_steps: Review item path, hidden status, signing, ownership, and user approval context.

title: macOS system extension observed
why_it_matters: System extensions can provide powerful endpoint capabilities and should be validated during compromise triage.
false_positive_notes: EDR, VPN, security, and device management tools commonly use system extensions.
recommended_next_steps: Validate Team ID, bundle path, approval state, and whether the extension is expected for the asset.

title: macOS TCC privacy grant observed
why_it_matters: TCC grants can expose sensitive data or control channels such as Accessibility and ScreenCapture.
false_positive_notes: Approved management, conferencing, and security tools may need TCC access.
recommended_next_steps: Review service, client, signing identity, timestamp, and user approval context.

title: Recent quarantined downloaded file
why_it_matters: Quarantine metadata can identify recently downloaded files relevant to initial access or payload staging.
false_positive_notes: Normal downloads carry quarantine metadata; validate file type, source URL, and execution history.
recommended_next_steps: Correlate quarantine paths with browser history, Gatekeeper/XProtect logs, and process execution.

title: Unsigned executable referenced by persistence
pattern: not signed|code object is not signed|TeamIdentifier=not set
why_it_matters: Unsigned executables in persistence context can indicate untrusted software or malware.
false_positive_notes: Internal scripts may be unsigned; validate path, owner, hash, quarantine, and business purpose.
recommended_next_steps: Preserve file metadata, hash, signing output, and the persistence artifact that references it.

title: Gatekeeper/XProtect/MRT event observed
why_it_matters: macOS security framework events may indicate suspicious downloads, blocks, scans, or malware detections.
false_positive_notes: Some messages are routine security scanning; validate the event detail and file path.
recommended_next_steps: Correlate with quarantine metadata, downloads, process execution, and persistence evidence.

title: macOS login item present
why_it_matters: Login items can provide user-level persistence and may hide unwanted software.
false_positive_notes: Many approved productivity and management apps use login items.
recommended_next_steps: Review path, hidden status, code signing, ownership, and background task management context.

title: LaunchServices handler or binding observed
why_it_matters: LaunchServices handlers can affect how files and URLs open and may support persistence or user interaction hijacking.
false_positive_notes: Most handlers are benign application registrations; validate unusual paths or unsigned handlers.
recommended_next_steps: Review handler path, bundle identity, signing state, and whether it aligns with installed applications.
